title: Stay-at-home requirements during the COVID-19 pandemic
subtitle: If policies vary at the subnational level, the index is shown as the response level of the strictest sub-region.
caption:
Stay-at-home requirements during the COVID-19 pandemic, Dec 31, 2022
If policies vary at the subnational level, the index is shown as the response level of the strictest sub-region.

This choropleth map displays the strictness of stay-at-home mandates across the globe on December 31, 2022, using a color scale that ranges from light blue for no measures to dark red for requirements with few exceptions. The vast majority of the world’s landmass and population falls into the dark blue category, representing countries where stay-at-home orders were required but allowed for essential activities. This dominant policy stance covers nearly all of South America, including Brazil and Argentina, as well as the entirety of Africa, Russia, China, and most of Europe, including France, Germany, and the United Kingdom. In contrast, a distinct cluster of nations in South Asia appears in orange, indicating a stricter tier where requirements allowed for few exceptions; this group includes India, Bangladesh, and Myanmar. A smaller set of countries, including the United States, Canada, Greenland, and South Korea, is shaded in light blue, signifying that stay-at-home measures were merely recommended rather than legally required. Australia and New Zealand align with the global majority in the dark blue band. The map shows a clear geographic concentration of the strictest visible policies in South Asia, while the rest of the world largely converged on a uniform level of restriction that permitted essential movement. The data presents a global landscape where mandatory stay-at-home orders with essential exceptions were the standard, with only a few specific regions deviating toward either stricter enforcement or voluntary recommendations.
